_meta.what_firm_does: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that builds Claude; its mission is to ensure the world safely makes the transition through transformative AI. The Human Data team manages the experts and contributors whose work shapes Claude's capabilities.
_meta.jd_wants: An operations manager to run programs for a distributed contributor population — clean onboarding, data integrity, vendor coordination, and turning operational signal into decisions across cross-functional partners.
_meta.status: DRAFT — Jamie to review/edit before submit

Why are you interested in this role?: Program operations isn't just about keeping a tracker updated—from my experience, it's a continuous cycle of onboarding people cleanly, protecting data integrity, and turning operational signal into decisions through cross-functional collaboration. That's the daily reality I already live. At InGenius Prep I own the end-to-end onboarding workflows and lifecycles for 20+ concurrent programs, acting as the primary liaison across Marketing, Tech, and Sales and 10+ vendors—reconciling records, holding data integrity, and refining delivery as we scale. Standing up operations for a distributed, fast-moving contributor population is exactly that work, and doing it for the people who shape Claude's capabilities makes the operational rigor feel genuinely worth getting right.
Why Anthropic?: Two honest reasons. First, the mission—building safe, beneficial AI—is work I'd be glad to support operationally; I use AI tools daily, so I care about how this technology gets built carefully. Second, the Human Data team sits exactly where my strengths are: the experts who shape Claude's capabilities need clean onboarding, reliable data, and someone who turns the operational mess into clear decisions. I'd rather be the dependable operational backbone for a team doing work this consequential than keep programs ticking somewhere the stakes are lower. The combination of high care and high pace is the environment I do my best work in.
What excites you about this opportunity?: What excites me is that this role treats operations as load-bearing, not administrative. With a distributed contributor population and data that directly shapes a model, getting onboarding, records, and integrity right isn't bureaucracy—it's the foundation everything else rests on. At Vestas I directed an M&A project migrating and merging employee records with zero tolerance for error, and coordinated immigration and relocation across attorneys and internal teams, which sharpened my judgment on prioritization, escalation, and being the reliable point of contact when things get complicated. The chance to build that kind of dependable operational system for the Human Data team, while it's still being shaped, is the part I'd look forward to most.
Tell us about a time you turned operatio: At ODN Oregon, an HR team was carrying a $362K leave-cost problem and assumed it was unavoidable. Rather than hand them an answer, I upskilled the team to audit 300+ confidential leave cases themselves, surface what the data actually showed, and refute the cost assumptions with evidence—which led to real policy redesigns and first-aid training. The operational work (the audit, the records, the integrity of the data) was what made the decision credible. It taught me how much it matters to move a conversation from assumptions to evidence, which is exactly how my MS in Applied Organizational Psychology trained me to operate: diagnose with data, collaborate cross-functionally on the fix, then track whether progress holds.
